results.finalAnswer:
ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Components/Api/Application/Services/ApplicationService.php - service-layer orchestration is the safest first stop for HTTP request behavior in this codebase's "fat services" structure
SUBSYSTEM: app/Components/Api/Application/Entity/Application.php - high-fan hub model likely coupled to request payload persistence, relations, and downstream side effects
SUBSYSTEM: tests/CreatesApplication.php - test bootstrap is the fastest place to anchor regression coverage before changing request handling

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Providers/RouteServiceProvider.php - wires `api`, `api/v2`, and `api/v3` plus request rate limiters, so it is the top-level request dispatch map
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Kernel.php - applies global and route middleware for CORS, auth/token setup, throttling, Sentry context, and request logging before controllers run
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Requests - holds the FormRequest validation/authorization layer, the safest place to change accepted input without leaking logic into controllers

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Providers/RouteServiceProvider.php - maps `api`, `api/v2`, and `api/v3` plus central rate-limit definitions, so versioned request entry fans out here
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Kernel.php - defines the global and `api` middleware stacks, including token auth, user hydration, logging, and throttling
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Requests - this is the safest boundary for request-contract changes because FormRequests handle authorization, validation, and payload shaping before thin controllers call services

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Kernel.php - middleware order and aliases for CORS, auth, token checks, logging, and throttling are defined here
SUBSYSTEM: app/Providers/RouteServiceProvider.php - API v1/v2/v3 are mounted here and custom rate limiters are registered here, so versioned request flow branches here
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Requests - FormRequest classes centralize validation and per-endpoint authorization, making this the safest place to change accepted input

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Providers/RouteServiceProvider.php - mounts `/api`, `/api/v2`, and `/api/v3` and wires in request rate limiters, so versioned request flow starts here
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Kernel.php - defines the global and API middleware chain for auth, throttling, Sentry context, and request logging
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Requests - holds FormRequest authorization and validation, the safest place to change input contracts before thin controllers delegate onward

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Providers/RouteServiceProvider.php - mounts the versioned API route trees and defines named rate limiters, so scope and rollout of request changes start here
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Kernel.php - composes the global and API middleware pipeline, so auth, throttling, logging, CORS, and request ordering are controlled here
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Requests - FormRequest classes own endpoint-level authorization, validation, and input shaping, making this the safest place for request-contract changes
